title: (No AI) AI ‘actor’ Tilly Norwood put out the worst song I’ve ever heard
dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
content: This song is an AI actor's rallying cry to other AI actors, urging them to keep going despite the naysayers who doubt their humanity. Literally no one can relate to this.

title: (No AI) One of Grammarly’s ‘experts’ is suing the company over its identity-stealing AI feature
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/gettyimages-1244428289.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Journalist Julia Angwin is one of the writers whose likeness was used in Grammarly’s “expert review” feature. | Photo: Eóin Noonan / Sportsfile via Getty Images

For months, Grammarly has been using the identities of real people (including us) for its ["Expert Review" AI suggestions](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/890921/grammarly-ai-expert-reviews) without getting their permission, and now it's facing a lawsuit from one of the journalists included, as previously reported by [_Wired_](https://www.wired.com/story/grammarly-is-facing-a-class-action-lawsuit-over-its-ai-expert-review-feature/). The [class-action complaint](https://static1.squarespace.com/static/63ffda7f9dcd742dc8620173/t/69b1ca7b00a832589866da3a/1773259387652/Grammarly+Complaint+%28Angwin+v.+Superhuman+Platform%2C+Inc.%29.pdf) filed by journalist Julia Angwin on Wednesday alleges that Superhuman violated the "experts'" privacy and publicity rights by breaking laws against using someone's identity for commercial purposes without their consent.

Angwin says she found out her identity was used by [way of Casey Newton](https://www.platformer.news/grammarly-expert-review-reviewed/), who is also one of the experts that _The Verge_ uncovered being used by Gra …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/893451/grammarly-ai-lawsuit-julia-angwin)
contentSnippet:
Journalist Julia Angwin is one of the writers whose likeness was used in Grammarly’s “expert review” feature. | Photo: Eóin Noonan / Sportsfile via Getty Images	

For months, Grammarly has been using the identities of real people (including us) for its "Expert Review" AI suggestions without getting their permission, and now it's facing a lawsuit from one of the journalists included, as previously reported by Wired. The class-action complaint filed by journalist Julia Angwin on Wednesday alleges that Superhuman violated the "experts'" privacy and publicity rights by breaking laws against using someone's identity for commercial purposes without their consent.
Angwin says she found out her identity was used by way of Casey Newton, who is also one of the experts that The Verge uncovered being used by Gra …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: For months, Grammarly has been using the identities of real people (including us) for its "Expert Review" AI suggestions without getting their permission, and now it's facing a lawsuit from one of the journalists included, as previously reported by Wired. The class-action complaint filed by journalist Julia Angwin on Wednesday alleges that Superhuman violated the […]

title: (No AI) Grammarly says it will stop using AI to clone experts without permission
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![A screenshot of a draft Verge post in Google Docs with an AI-generated Grammarly comment using Nilay Patel’s name](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/grammr-crop.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Superhuman says it has disabled Grammarly's "expert review" AI feature that said its edit suggestions were "inspired by" real writers, [including our editor-in-chief](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/890921/grammarly-ai-expert-reviews) and other _Verge_ staff members.

"After careful consideration, we have decided to disable Expert Review as we reimagine the feature to make it more useful for users, while giving experts real control over how they want to be represented - or not represented at all," Ailian Gan, Superhuman's director of product management, said in a statement to _The Verge_. "Based on the feedback we've received, we clearly missed the mark. We are sorry and will do things differently going forward."

…

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/893270/grammarly-ai-expert-review-disabled)
contentSnippet:

"Based on the feedback we've received, we clearly missed the mark. We are sorry and will do things differently going forward." 
 …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Miles Klee
title: (No AI) Grammarly Is Facing a Class Action Lawsuit Over Its AI ‘Expert Review’ Feature
dc:creator: Miles Klee
content: The feature, which Grammarly shut down Wednesday, presented editing suggestions as if they came from established authors and academics—without their consent.
contentSnippet: The feature, which Grammarly shut down Wednesday, presented editing suggestions as if they came from established authors and academics—without their consent.
